229661.explanation: D66 Borne supports citizen assemblies, as these lead to more understanding, support, and better decisions than a simple yes/no referendum.

229663.explanation: Collaboration is necessary for Borne to successfully implement all plans.

229664.explanation: More flexibility is fine, but we must ensure opening hours are appropriate for Borne.

229665.explanation: As long as it is a secondary activity, it is already allowed: we are pleased with that. If it becomes a main activity, existing rules should remain in force as far as we're concerned.

229666.explanation: Generating and consuming local energy together helps reduce problems on our electricity grid. We all benefit from this.

229667.explanation: That is still a legal requirement; as long as that does not change, we comply with it.

229668.explanation: Helping newcomers integrate means they can work faster and contribute to our society. Good for newcomers and good for Borne.

229669.explanation: Within the built-up area, open fireplaces cause most nuisance.

229670.explanation: We want to encourage companies to work socially and sustainably. Therefore, we want to make it attractive for such companies to settle in Borne: good establishment conditions, provided they meet the requirements.

229671.explanation: We strongly prefer solar panels on roofs. For small projects, for example on leftover plots that still have an agricultural designation, we do not exclude solar on land.

229672.explanation: Accessibility in public spaces and buildings is a priority for D66 Borne. We want the municipality to facilitate and encourage entrepreneurs to this end. In exceptional cases, this can involve financial contributions.

229673.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in our society! We want to work with our residents to remove barriers as best as possible. Paying a membership fee can be part of this.

229675.explanation: Attention to our Twentse culture is good: it helps children form ties with our village and region. Familiarity with Twents is part of that.

229676.explanation: We gladly support our associations, but this should not simply allow associations to always ask for handouts.

229677.explanation: Too little has been invested in enforcement in Borne in recent years. Rules – which apply to everyone – are only credible with enforcement.

229678.explanation: This is an important principle, but we must take good account of a proper mix in all streets and neighborhoods. Therefore, we look not per construction plan but at the existing environment.

229679.explanation: Of course, this is not suitable everywhere, but at locations to be determined. It is a valuable and space-saving way of building.

229680.explanation: Experiences from across the country show that this causes obstacles for existing businesses.

229681.explanation: D66 Borne is not in favor of abolishing all aesthetic regulations. However, we want to look at simplifying them in good consultation with the city architect.

229683.explanation: Currently, too many people are being left out.

229684.explanation: Everyone wants to be seen, to matter, and to contribute. D66 Borne finds it important that this contribution is according to ability. We want each case to be assessed for what is appropriate and possible.

229685.explanation: Everyone wants to matter and contribute to their environment. Work is a very important way to do this.

229687.explanation: Our center is disfigured by too much steel on the street. We want to relocate parking to the edges of the center. This will make our center much more attractive!

229689.explanation: The share of electric cars is increasing. The number of charging points must keep pace with this, while the municipality should primarily encourage charging on private property.

229691.explanation: D66 Borne supports good cycling and walking routes. The F35 is an important facility that makes cycling more attractive. The route to Almelo is important!

229692.explanation: When our entire core shopping area is car-free, it is much safer and more pleasant for visitors to shop and visit the terraces.

229693.explanation: For D66 Borne, safety is extremely important. 30 km/h speeds contribute greatly to that. It makes cycling and walking more enjoyable and safer. We make an exception for through roads with separate bike paths.

229696.explanation: Many of our residents come by bike, fantastic! Good, secure places to park these bicycles make cycling more attractive and also ensure the streets are free of obstacles.

229698.explanation: The F35 is a cycle highway for continuous (fast!) bicycle traffic. The combination with the busy roundabout is highly unfortunate.

229699.explanation: Wind turbines in places where they cause no inconvenience are fine. Near and on Twickel, we think this is not a good idea.

229700.explanation: Overviews from across the country show that post-sorting leads to (much) higher costs for our residents.

229701.explanation: Plain grass is cheaper and above all healthier.

229705.explanation: This aligns with the wishes of the Zenderen village council.

229707.explanation: We find it important that newcomers can participate well and quickly in our society. The best route for this differs per newcomer. Sometimes something other than language lessons is needed first.

229710.explanation: Eventually, everyone will have to heat their home free from fossil energy.

229713.explanation: Quality over quantity. Continuity of care is important, and not every organization can deliver that.

229714.explanation: D66 Borne considers placing cameras in most situations far too heavy a measure; it can only be necessary in extreme cases. Our residents must be able to walk the streets unmonitored.

229661.explanation: VVD Borne finds it extremely important that residents are involved in important issues. We involve residents and entrepreneurs early in decision-making, even if the municipality does not yet know everything precisely. But we do not support a referendum.

229663.explanation: The municipality cannot do everything itself. Good service for our residents is paramount. Cooperation with our neighbors is therefore important.

229664.explanation: It is good to place responsibility with hospitality entrepreneurs. We want a vibrant center. However, there must be no structural nuisance for the neighborhood. Then limits must be set.

229665.explanation: Farmers must have the space to use their land for multiple purposes, such as tourism, agricultural nature management, recreation, or other business activities.

229666.explanation: We choose to keep peace in the wallets of our residents. After all, the money the municipality spends is also raised by the residents. The municipality already has schemes to help residents save energy. We want to keep those.

229667.explanation: Allocation must be based on urgency, connection to Borne, Hertme, and Zenderen, and an individual assessment. This means no priority for status holders.

229668.explanation: We already offer the support needed to integrate well. It is up to the newcomer to make use of this and to take responsibility, for example by learning Dutch quickly.

229669.explanation: The municipality should not want to regulate everything. Therefore, we do not want to ban the use of open fireplaces, wood stoves, or for example, barbecues or fire baskets.

229670.explanation: There are clear national rules that businesses must comply with. Providing space for entrepreneurship means that we locally should not impose additional rules.

229671.explanation: The installation of solar fields is only acceptable to us if carefully integrated into the landscape, such as alongside the A35. Agricultural land is excluded.

229672.explanation: It is up to entrepreneurs to decide how to run their business. Accessibility for people with disabilities is important. In practice, most entrepreneurs arrange this themselves.

229673.explanation: Sports should in principle be accessible to everyone, including people with low incomes. That is why we already have regulations in place. These will remain.

229675.explanation: The municipality does not govern this. This is up to the schools themselves.

229676.explanation: Sports clubs are responsible for their own club funds. We find it important that everyone can play sports. Therefore, we advocate for good municipal sports facilities such as the new sports center. These can then be rented by clubs at a favorable rate.

229677.explanation: The VVD wants more enforcement officers because where you live, it must be safe.

229678.explanation: For too many people, owning a home has become unattainable. VVD Borne commits to building sufficient affordable owner-occupied homes, and where necessary rental homes, for starters, families, young people, and seniors from Borne, Hertme, and Zenderen and from Twente.

229679.explanation: VVD Borne sees possibilities for high-rise buildings in Borne to a certain extent. As far as we are concerned, limited high-rise must fit into the existing environment and not reduce the quality of the living environment. The location is decisive here and for this.

229680.explanation: Business parks can be converted to residential areas to a limited extent, such as at the Jonge Poerink site near Oonksweg / Prins Bernhardlaan in Borne.

229681.explanation: VVD Borne focuses on simplifying the rules and speeding up procedures to realize construction projects faster.

229683.explanation: We already offer a lot of help in Borne to people who demonstrably need it.

229684.explanation: Work is more than just income; it involves social encounters, meaning, and personal development. We encourage residents dependent on welfare benefits to participate in society as much as possible. This means, where possible, doing (volunteer) work.

229685.explanation: Work is more than just income; it involves social encounters, meaning, and personal development. We encourage residents dependent on welfare benefits to participate in society as much as possible. You don't achieve this through subsidies.

229687.explanation: You should be able to park for free when doing groceries in the town center. Then you should also be able to find a parking spot. The number of free parking places needs to be expanded.

229689.explanation: Charging stations should be installed only where there is actual demand. But we also want people with non-electric cars to still be able to find a parking spot in their own street.

229691.explanation: A safe and fast cycling connection to Almelo is important for Borne. The F35 cycling highway between Borne and Almelo still needs to be constructed. Agreements about this have been made with the province and Almelo. We think it's important to honor these agreements.

229692.explanation: The center already has a clearly defined pedestrian zone. The center must remain conveniently accessible by car.

229693.explanation: VVD Borne chooses safe street design: 50 km/h where possible, 30 km/h where necessary.

229696.explanation: There are sufficient places in the center to safely park your bicycle.

229698.explanation: The F35 has just been constructed. Building it again would be a waste of capital, and there is no space available for the cycling highway on the other side of the railway.

229699.explanation: VVD Borne is against the placement of wind turbines in the beautiful Twente coulisse landscape. No wind turbines are possible in Borne. Additionally, we believe all available resources should be used to prevent wind turbines from being placed on or just beyond the municipal border.

229700.explanation: We choose source separation of waste if it is affordable for our residents.

229701.explanation: The football clubs are gradually outgrowing their facilities. Artificial turf can enable more frequent and extensive football playing.

229705.explanation: Housing initiatives, such as on the site of Zenderen Vooruit, deserve our support. A realistic plan should be made for this in consultation with the residents of Zenderen.

229707.explanation: We expect newcomers to make maximum effort to learn the Dutch language. Only then does integration into our society have a chance.

229710.explanation: Residents themselves should be able to decide how to heat their homes. We commit to feasible and affordable options.

229713.explanation: The number of providers is not important, but the quality they deliver and the price they ask.

229714.explanation: Camera surveillance should be deployed earlier to increase the chance of apprehension and to be able to monitor high-risk locations live. We will relax rules here if possible.

229661.explanation: Nuance is often lost in a referendum. There are better ways to encourage participation by residents, such as a motion market, a consultation evening, or a participatory budget.

229663.explanation: For certain services, it is better to cooperate to ensure the right quality and safety. This keeps the government effective and affordable. Our intention is to remain an independent municipality.

229664.explanation: Entrepreneurs can perfectly decide this themselves based on market demand.

229665.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to earn money with their farms. A store selling good products from local land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

229666.explanation: This is the ideal scenario: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the power on the same site, so the grid is minimally loaded. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be stimulated.

229667.explanation: There is currently great pressure on the housing market. We want a safe society where we can all live our lives. We want status holders to integrate as quickly as possible and be self-sufficient, and we believe a home is a basic condition for this.

229668.explanation: Integration of newcomers often does not happen automatically and is very important for a livable Borne for everyone.

229669.explanation: The municipality provides good information about responsible wood burning and imposes a ban on burning during unfavorable weather conditions. This ensures clean air and is a great relief for people with respiratory ailments.

229670.explanation: But sustainable working can be encouraged for all companies.

229671.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

229672.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

229673.explanation: Because everyone should be able to participate. And overall, society benefits if people are healthier.

229675.explanation: The Twents dialect is one of the defining parts of our identity, and it must not be lost.

229676.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

229677.explanation: Safety and enforcement are important for our society.

229678.explanation: We will continue to build affordable homes. Borne already builds 30% social rental homes and 40% affordable owner-occupied homes. We will continue this. We aim to maintain this balance between social rental and owner-occupied housing throughout the municipality.

229679.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and action is needed. With high-rise buildings, you can quickly add many homes. We say: build upwards near a good public transport connection. This way, we make good use of that valuable land.

229680.explanation: As long as safety and health are guaranteed, increasing the number of available homes should be possible.

229681.explanation: Not abolish, but we do stimulate (re)building without endless regulations, for example with flexible homes, shared housing, and caregiver homes.

229683.explanation: Currently, people who work but earn too little to cover their living expenses receive help from the municipality as long as they do not earn more than 120% of the welfare benefit standard. We believe this threshold should be raised because 120% proves insufficient to pay for basic needs.

229684.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

229685.explanation: As far as can be determined, there are sufficient jobs for the demand that exists.

229687.explanation: In the new environmental vision, car traffic in the center is discouraged; thus, more parking spaces would be unnecessary. It must remain accessible for entrepreneurs and people with mobility impairments.

229689.explanation: The supply already grows along with demand, and that is fine.

229691.explanation: The construction of the F35 cycling highway to Almelo must be accelerated. We want to make it easier for cyclists. Therefore, we want a rapid expansion of the F35. The longer we wait, the higher the costs become.

229692.explanation: We advocate for a clear separation between traffic and pedestrians. We will make the center of Borne more attractive for shoppers by creating a low-traffic section of Grotestraat.

229693.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

229696.explanation: Sufficient safe bicycle parking spaces are important. For example, in the village center and other locations that attract many cyclists. This encourages cycling instead of car use.

229698.explanation: The F35 has recently been fully constructed in the village. Changing that now would mean wasting a lot of money.

229699.explanation: We remain supporters of wind energy and welcome sustainable energy generation within the municipality. We aim for partly local ownership. Also small wind turbines (

229700.explanation: In cooperation with TwenteMilieu and Twence we are exploring options to eventually make this possible. Until then, the municipality supports residents in properly sorting their waste.

229705.explanation: This is not automatically the right choice for Zenderen. There are other possibilities as well. These must first be thoroughly researched and discussed.

229707.explanation: Status holders should start internships or (volunteer) work as soon as possible so they can learn the Dutch language faster. Participating means joining in.

229714.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

229661.explanation: The SP-Borne strongly supports a referendum; this promotes citizen input and participation.

229663.explanation: Small municipalities do not have all specialist knowledge in-house. Therefore, in some areas they can better seek cooperation with larger municipalities in the region.

229664.explanation: But there must be a strict closing time applied based on the granted permit.

229665.explanation: It must fit in with the surroundings.

229666.explanation: But the municipality is allowed to assist with applying for subsidies.

229667.explanation: However, they must remain part of the so-called priority groups.

229668.explanation: The government, companies, and education are responsible for this.

229669.explanation: We do support the stove guide (do not burn during orange and red codes) and the stove’s flue pipe should be equipped with a fine particle filter.

229670.explanation: Because what exactly is meant by sustainable in this context? First, criteria are needed so companies know what requirements they must meet.

229671.explanation: Because they represent a major intrusion in the landscape. Preferably small wind turbines for own energy needs, such as on farms.

229672.explanation: That is part of the responsibility of entrepreneurs.

229673.explanation: But then in kind, based on the Sociaal Fonds regulations in Borne.

229675.explanation: As part of cultural knowledge, this is fine. Learn the history of the region you live in. But it should not be mandatory.

229676.explanation: Only when the association proves sufficiently viable and clearly meets a need.

229677.explanation: And especially in the context of preventive supervision.

229678.explanation: The municipality of Borne must meet the 30%. In recent new construction projects, the number of social rental homes has been too minimal.

229679.explanation: Only if this high-rise fits in the environment and has sufficient distance from low-rise buildings.

229680.explanation: When business parks become vacant, homes should be allowed to be built, and on existing industrial sites preferably only company housing.

229681.explanation: Regulations can be simplified, without causing uncontrolled growth.

229683.explanation: Due to sharply rising prices of e.g. groceries and energy, financial problems are occurring among a larger group of people. Therefore, SP-Borne supports raising the income limit to 130% of the social minimum.

229685.explanation: Long-term unemployed can also regain work rhythm by, for example, doing volunteer work.

229687.explanation: In principle, there are enough parking spaces, but there is tension between short-term parking by visitors and long-term parking by those working in or around the center.

229689.explanation: There are enough. So far, electric cars are only affordable for people with a middle to high income. Also, there are good possibilities to install a charging station at one’s own home.

229691.explanation: Because the municipality also receives subsidies for this from the Province/the State. Additionally, it encourages less car use.

229692.explanation: We do not want to lock down the center, but at certain points adjustments can be considered to make streets low-traffic.

229693.explanation: With the exception of main roads. Moreover, it is proven that this measure increases traffic safety.

229696.explanation: This improves safety for both bicycles and cyclists and helps prevent a cluttered appearance. It also has a stimulating effect.

229698.explanation: It is inevitable that urban and village centers will partly guide the bicycle highway over existing roads. Moreover, this is too disruptive for residents on that side of the railway and very costly.

229699.explanation: The municipality should primarily think constructively about where wind turbines can be placed together with surrounding municipalities.

229700.explanation: Because this causes citizens to lose awareness about dealing with waste. The municipality should mainly make it as easy as possible for citizens to deliver waste separately.

229701.explanation: Sports clubs should assess themselves whether artificial turf is desired or necessary for their sport. Artificial turf fields also have disadvantages for some sports/athletes.

229705.explanation: If the community of Zenderen is in the majority in favor of this.

229707.explanation: The municipality should organize language lessons itself or via a non-profit partner and make attendance mandatory, but should not impose income-based sanctions. That neither promotes nor stimulates integration.

229710.explanation: Often the energy choice for heating is already determined by the existing infrastructure of energy supply options.

229713.explanation: Because the recurring tendering process causes great unrest among clients and care staff and harms the quality and continuity of support.

229714.explanation: It is not useful to just hang cameras everywhere. First, the nature and extent of the nuisance must be determined and how to respond to it.

229661.explanation: Residents should be able to give their opinion on important topics, but a referendum is not a good tool for this. It reduces a complex question to a simple yes or no. GB90 chooses participation from the start so that choices are well considered and supported.

229663.explanation: Borne remains an independent municipality with a strong Born identity. Smart cooperation yields more results at lower costs, while Borne can still determine its own course.

229664.explanation: GB90 advocates for extended opening hours and more opportunities for hospitality and events. We also pay attention to the interests of residents nearby. Together we make agreements about extended opening hours and possibilities.

229665.explanation: We give farmers the space to keep their businesses financially healthy. At the same time, we protect our rural area and make clear choices together about what is and is not allowed.

229666.explanation: We welcome jointly generating sustainable energy. Many national subsidies are already available for this. Additional financial support is not necessary.

229667.explanation: Municipalities are legally obliged to provide housing for status holders. Instead of automatic priority, GB90 chooses an alternative: several single status holders share a home together. This way, we ensure we use social rental homes sparingly.

229668.explanation: By involving people from day one, they become independent faster and increase their chances of work. Good guidance is necessary for this. No extra money is currently needed.

229669.explanation: Residents are themselves responsible for their heating behavior and take others into account. The Heating Guide can help residents with this.

229670.explanation: Entrepreneurs who (still) do not work sustainably also provide employment and services that are important to residents.

229671.explanation: We do not place solar panels on good agricultural land. The province also prohibits this.

229672.explanation: Shops and community buildings must be accessible to everyone. GB90 wants to stimulate associations and entrepreneurs with a small contribution where needed. Entrepreneurs basically have their own responsibility in this.

229673.explanation: Sports must be accessible to all children and adults, even if people have little to spend.

229675.explanation: Attention to Twents dialect, local traditions, and history in primary school is valuable. Schools themselves decide how to implement this.

229676.explanation: Good sports facilities and vital associations are important. Associations are responsible for their own finances. If problems arise, we look for tailor-made solutions.

229677.explanation: Good enforcement is important. Borne has enough public order officers (BOAs). However, the visibility of BOAs and police must improve.

229678.explanation: In new construction, at least 30% must be social rental housing. This is a legal obligation. We comply with this.

229679.explanation: High-rise buildings offer homes where there is a high demand and use relatively little space. Sometimes it fits well, such as along the railway or in large new construction projects. Sometimes it does not, like next to Villa Elisabeth (Dorset). Therefore, we carefully assess at each location whether high-rise is appropriate.

229680.explanation: Building regular homes on an industrial estate is undesirable and not allowed. The law clearly prescribes this.

229681.explanation: Rules can be relaxed, but completely abolishing them goes too far. These rules prevent extreme situations, such as a bright pink house. This way we ensure the neighborhood remains beautiful and pleasant for everyone.

229683.explanation: The current threshold for minimum schemes is 120% of the social minimum. Instead of raising this threshold, GB90 focuses on more customization so the schemes better match what people really need to participate fully.

229684.explanation: It is important that people on social assistance participate and make a meaningful contribution to society. This can be done in various ways. We take everyone's abilities and personal situation into account.

229685.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities to help people who have been unemployed for a long time to find work.

229687.explanation: The parking pressure in and around the center is increasing. More parking spaces are needed so that shops and facilities remain easily accessible and the center remains pleasant and attractive for everyone.

229689.explanation: Currently, enough places are designated. The need must be monitored.

229691.explanation: The province pays most of the costs for this part of the F35 cycling highway. We are positive about the regional F35 project. Borne's contribution is limited.

229692.explanation: GB90 supports a low-car center. A car-free center is difficult, costly, and not good for entrepreneurs in the center.

229696.explanation: In Borne it must become easier and safer to park your bike. An expensive, fully secured bike parking is not feasible. Therefore, we focus on practical, affordable solutions.

229698.explanation: We will not waste money on relocating a newly constructed F35.

229699.explanation: Wind energy does not fit near residential areas or vulnerable nature. Therefore, there is no place for wind turbines in Borne.

229700.explanation: We will investigate the possibilities of post-separation. Switching now to a post-separation system is not yet possible.

229701.explanation: Good sports facilities are important. Investing requires customization. This also applies to whether or not to have artificial turf.

229705.explanation: Housing development in Zenderen is important so that young and old can continue living in their own village. This keeps the village lively and helps us maintain important facilities, such as the primary school, for the entire community.

229707.explanation: Everyone participates. Participation starts with learning the Dutch language, so people can contribute to society and find work. Therefore, the municipality can, taking individual circumstances into account, reduce welfare benefits if a newcomer does not attend language classes without a valid reason.

229710.explanation: Homeowners decide for themselves how to heat their homes. We focus on practical solutions that can be applied immediately and provide people with lower energy savings, comfort, and freedom. A heat network is not an option at this time.

229713.explanation: Choosing fewer providers means that many elderly will lose their regular help and the job security of many household assistants is at risk. That is why we do not opt for this.

229714.explanation: We deploy security cameras in places where they genuinely contribute to safety and nuisance control. This makes our public spaces safer and more pleasant to live in.

229661.explanation: BorneNu stands for direct democracy. For major decisions, residents should be able to express their opinion via a referendum. Not everywhere or always, but on matters that truly affect the living environment and future of Borne.

229663.explanation: Collaboration is sometimes necessary, but not automatic. BorneNu wants decisions to be made as close to residents as possible. Regional cooperation only if it demonstrably adds value and is democratically controllable.

229664.explanation: Entrepreneurship is important, but not at the expense of livability. Completely unrestricted opening hours increase nuisance and pressure on enforcement. BorneNu chooses clear agreements and customization within clear frameworks.

229665.explanation: BorneNu supports agricultural entrepreneurship, but 'always allowing' goes too far. Secondary activities require customization and must fit the landscape, environment, and zoning plan.

229666.explanation: BorneNu supports small-scale, well-integrated initiatives such as joint solar panels, insulation, or green roofs. No large-scale projects that damage the landscape. Residents remain owners.

229667.explanation: Housing is necessary, but not at the expense of first-time buyers and Borne residents. BorneNu chooses equal opportunities and acceleration of housing construction.

229668.explanation: Integration is important, but Borne already invests a lot in this. Additional budget is not needed. Participation, learning the language, and working also require effort from newcomers themselves.

229669.explanation: Freedom of choice remains, provided it is used responsibly. BorneNu chooses education and intervention in case of nuisance. Prohibition is too harsh; steering towards conscious use works better.

229670.explanation: Stimulating sustainability, not excluding. Companies in transition must also remain welcome. This way the municipality retains influence and entrepreneurship remains possible.

229671.explanation: Agricultural land is for agriculture. Solar panels should first be placed on roofs and business parks, and only then look further.

229672.explanation: Accessibility is a basic requirement. Municipal support helps entrepreneurs and ensures everyone can participate, regardless of disability.

229673.explanation: Everyone should be able to play sports, but through customization and existing funds, especially for youth. This ensures help reaches where it is needed and remains affordable.

229675.explanation: Twents is cultural heritage. Giving attention in a playful way strengthens identity and connection. Encourage, not oblige.

229676.explanation: Sports clubs are a social binding agent. Temporary, targeted support combined with a recovery plan keeps clubs afloat.

229677.explanation: Visible enforcement works preventively. More community officers increase safety and approachability, as a supplement to the police.

229678.explanation: More social housing is needed for first-time buyers and lower incomes. 30% keeps Borne socially balanced, provided housing construction speeds up.

229679.explanation: High-rise apartment blocks do not fit the village character. BorneNu chooses low to medium-height building with attention to greenery and livability.

229680.explanation: Business parks are necessary for employment. Mixing living and working leads to conflicts. Housing belongs elsewhere.

229681.explanation: Aesthetic regulations ensure quality and coherence. BorneNu wants to simplify and clarify them, but not abolish. Without rules, messy construction and damage to the village image threaten.

229683.explanation: A generic increase is not always effective. BorneNu chooses customization and follows national guidelines. This way we help people with real problems without unnecessarily stretching the system.

229684.explanation: Participating according to ability increases self-esteem and opportunities. The work must fit the situation and not displace regular work. Not punishment, but a stepping stone with guidance.

229685.explanation: BorneNu wants to guide people towards regular work. Too many subsidized jobs cause dependency. Education, guidance and cooperation with employers deliver more sustainable results.

229687.explanation: Accessibility is crucial for a lively center. BorneNu wants sufficient parking spaces, smartly integrated and well utilized. This helps entrepreneurs, the elderly, and visitors.

229689.explanation: The need differs per neighborhood. BorneNu wants to monitor first and expand in phases. Do not remove parking spaces unnecessarily without a clear demand.

229691.explanation: Good cycling routes are important, but expansion of the F35 is costly and mainly regionally relevant. No automatic municipal contribution.

229692.explanation: Extra car-free streets limit accessibility for entrepreneurs and residents. Improving stay quality can also be done without closing streets to traffic.

229693.explanation: 30 km/h increases safety and reduces noise. Implement logically: quieter residential streets, main roads continue to flow.

229696.explanation: Too large-scale and costly for Borne. BorneNu chooses visible parking facilities, lighting and supervision. That better fits the scale of the village.

229698.explanation: Relocating is drastic and costly, with uncertain added value. BorneNu opts to improve the existing route.

229699.explanation: Sustainability yes, but not at the expense of landscape and livability. First solar on roofs and alternatives.

229700.explanation: Post-separation can be more efficient without extra burden for residents. BorneNu chooses what demonstrably works and is affordable.

229701.explanation: No standard choice. Customization per sport and location, together with clubs.

229705.explanation: BorneNu is open to housing construction, provided it is careful and in consultation. The sports function must be maintained or equally compensated.

229707.explanation: Language is the key to participation. Anyone who refuses without a valid reason can be sanctioned. Help and responsibility go hand in hand.

229710.explanation: Encourage sustainability, not enforce. The municipality helps and advises, residents retain control over their homes.

229713.explanation: Fewer providers offer more clarity, better quality, and less risk of fraud. This benefits both clients and employees.

229714.explanation: Cameras can be effective in cases of serious nuisance. They should be used temporarily and targeted, combined with enforcement and care.

229661.explanation: The CDA considers participation important, but a referendum is not always the best tool. Sometimes it leads to high costs or complexity. We choose the form per topic that generates the most support.

229663.explanation: The CDA opts for smart collaboration in Twente where it is more efficient and better, such as in waste collection, fire services, healthcare, and safety. What can be done locally should remain with Borne; what works better together, we do together.

229664.explanation: The CDA supports extended opening hours for hospitality venues in the center.

229665.explanation: The CDA wants to give farmers space to make their businesses future-proof, for example with farm shops or small-scale campsites. Initiatives that fit circular agriculture and local products strengthen the countryside and deserve support.

229666.explanation: Stimulating sustainable energy is important to the CDA. Energy cooperatives where residents generate power together strengthen involvement and support. Therefore, the CDA believes the municipality may financially support such local initiatives.

229667.explanation: The CDA wants clear and fair criteria for allocating social rental homes. Vulnerable groups, including asylum recipients, should be helped, but always within a transparent system that does justice to all housing seekers.

229668.explanation: The CDA sees integration as an obligation that costs money, also in Borne. If more funds are needed, we will provide them, because not integrating and not truly participating in our community is a far bigger problem.

229669.explanation: The CDA believes wood burning should remain possible with common sense. There must be clear rules about when it is not allowed to prevent nuisance, pollution, and health problems.

229670.explanation: The CDA wants to encourage sustainable businesses but not create barriers for (starting) entrepreneurs. Strict admission requirements do not help innovation. We focus on supporting and encouraging sustainability, not exclusion.

229671.explanation: The CDA believes that good agricultural land should remain used for food production. Solar panels should be placed first on roofs and business buildings. This way, we use our space wisely and protect the agricultural landscape.

229672.explanation: The CDA believes everyone counts and should be able to participate independently in our community. Accessible buildings are essential for this. Therefore, the CDA wants to actively promote and improve accessibility where necessary.

229673.explanation: The CDA supports affordable sports for everyone. Everyone should be able to participate. Borne has a sports fund, and it should remain. Stimulating sports and exercise is important.

229675.explanation: The CDA sees attention to the Twents dialect as a valuable cultural enrichment for the youth. Traditions are important for our identity and neighborliness, and the Twents language is an explicit part of that.

229676.explanation: Sports and exercise for everyone is a priority for the CDA. Clubs should be able to use facilities under favorable conditions. Professionalization steps, such as hiring professionals, may be strengthened with targeted municipal support.

229677.explanation: The CDA stands for a safe Borne and a safe living environment. We must continually assess what is needed for this. Sufficient community officers (BOA’s) are important to be visibly present, address nuisance, and enhance livability.

229678.explanation: Affordable housing is a priority for the CDA. We have arranged that 30% social rental housing is part of the housing development approach in Borne. With the 30-40-30 policy, we ensure a healthy mix of social rental, mid-range rental, and homeownership.

229679.explanation: To build affordably, limited high-rise between 4 and 6 floors may be allowed, but only where it fits well in the environment. The CDA chooses affordability as well as retention of a human scale and spatial quality.

229680.explanation: The CDA believes business parks are primarily intended for companies; they are designed accordingly and residential use usually does not fit. In mixed zones where living and working are already combined, housing can be appropriate.

229681.explanation: The CDA wants affordable housing but also quality building. Aesthetic regulations help make sustainable, future-proof, and fitting construction possible. Abolishing them is unwise; simplifying where possible is preferable.

229683.explanation: Living costs are rising and the CDA consciously opts for a robust social safety net. If residents fall into trouble due to rising costs, the municipality must be able to help. Therefore, it is wise to increase the income threshold for support where necessary.

229684.explanation: The CDA supports a generous social safety net, but also participation. Those who can should contribute through socially meaningful work. This strengthens self-confidence, social participation, and aids reintegration.

229685.explanation: Long-term unemployed often have few opportunities in the labor market. The CDA believes we should help people get back to work. Subsidized jobs can be a good, temporary step towards regular employment.

229687.explanation: Borne is growing, and a lively center requires good accessibility. The CDA considers enough free parking spaces important so residents and visitors can easily do their shopping and the center remains attractive.

229689.explanation: Electric driving is increasing and therefore more charging points are needed. The CDA finds this logical, but they should not come at the expense of important facilities like disabled parking spaces. Smart placement and combination are important.

229691.explanation: Cycling for commuting and schoolchildren is a priority for the CDA. Investing in the F35 fits this. If the expansion lies on Borne territory, it is logical that Borne contributes financially to encourage safe and sustainable traffic.

229692.explanation: Part of the center is car-free and will remain so. Other parts remain accessible for shops and parking. With good design and appropriate measures, safety for all road users can be ensured.

229693.explanation: Traffic safety is a priority for the CDA. A maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas is a good standard to protect vulnerable road users and increase livability in residential neighborhoods.

229696.explanation: Bicycle theft is a problem, also in Borne. Secured bicycle parking in the center and at the station helps prevent theft and encourages cycling. The CDA sees this as a good investment in safety and accessibility.

229698.explanation: The F35 near the station was recently constructed. For the CDA, the priority is now the missing connection toward Almelo and a safe underpass under the railway. Relocating it is unnecessary and costly.

229699.explanation: The CDA opposes large wind turbines on Borne territory and along municipal borders. We will object to this at the provincial level. However, we want to consider small farm windmills as long as they fit the landscape.

229700.explanation: The CDA wants to continue waste separation where it works well, such as paper, glass, and garden waste. At the same time, research should be done into whether sorting packaging and residual waste afterwards can be sustainable and effective. The goal is more reuse of materials, not blindly changing the system.

229701.explanation: In Borne, sports clubs partly already have artificial turf and are satisfied with it. They ask for expansion, and the CDA believes we should invest in this. Artificial turf increases playability and supports a strong sports climate.

229705.explanation: The village council of Zenderen wants homes on the current sports field, combined with a new sports park along the future connecting road. The CDA sees this as an important development that strengthens Zenderen's livability and future.

229707.explanation: The CDA believes integration is essential for participation in society. Learning Dutch is an inseparable part. It is not optional: those who do not cooperate with language classes must be addressed, including consequences for benefits.

229710.explanation: Owners must keep freedom in their choices, but the municipality can promote what is sensible for energy saving. Rules are needed against nuisance from wood burning. In new construction, options like district heating or gas-free building can be chosen.

229713.explanation: Borne carefully chooses the best providers of household help so residents get tailored support. The CDA believes quality comes first. Therefore, we do not want fewer providers now; freedom of choice and appropriate support remain important.

229714.explanation: Crime and vandalism must be tackled decisively. Good lighting, enforcement, and cameras where necessary make places safer. The CDA chooses an overall package of measures, always respecting residents’ privacy.

statements.title: The residents of the municipality of Borne should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van de gemeente Borne moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants in the center should be able to set their own opening hours.
The municipality should encourage primary schools to include attention to Twents.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: A residential neighborhood should be built on the site of the Zenderen Vooruit sports club.
Owners of existing homes should be free to decide how they heat their house.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must abolish the aesthetic standards for building and renovating.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality of Borne should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.text: In de gemeente Borne moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Housing should also be built on business parks.
There should be many more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in the municipality of Borne.

titleParts.glossary: Government-funded jobs for people who have little chance of getting a regular job.
People who have been without paid work for a long time (longer than a year).

statements.title: The sports fields should have artificial turf instead of natural grass.
The municipality should contribute financially in the coming council term to the expansion of the F35 cycle highway between Borne and Almelo.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: meebetalen aan de uitbreiding van de fietssnelweg F35 tussen Borne en Almelo.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: More car-free streets should be created in Borne’s center.
The municipality should oppose new wind turbines at the provincial level.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zich bij de provincie verzetten tegen nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: A secure bicycle parking facility should be installed in the center.
The municipality should switch to a system where household waste is sorted only after it has been deposited.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should sign a contract with fewer different providers of domestic help.
The municipality should reduce the social assistance benefits of newcomers who do not attend language classes.

titleParts.glossary: People who are new to the Netherlands.
Giving less money or reducing a monetary amount.
Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should handle more itself and participate less in partnerships with other municipalities.
Where there is nuisance, a security camera should be installed quickly.

shootoutStatements.title: Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.
In the municipality of Borne the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.
In Borne residential apartment buildings of up to six storeys may be built.
The municipality should pay sports club membership fees for people with low incomes.
At least 30% of the newly built homes in the municipality of Borne must be social rental housing. Currently about 22% of all homes in the municipality are social rentals.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must move the F35 cycle highway at the station to the other side of the railway tracks.
